108.reason: [The character class [|&] only matches a single '|' or '&', but fails to detect cases where these appear outside valid contexts like "&&" or "||". However, the bigger issue is that this pattern may not correctly capture all illegal standalone '&' or '|' because the regex engine prioritizes longer matches; since "&&" and "||" are matched earlier, a single '&' or '|' will fall through to ILLEGAL. BUT: the pattern [|&] is correct for matching single | or &. The real bug is in error message formatting.]

226.reason: [The error type string includes a leading space (" a"), but the expected output format requires just "a". This causes the output to be "line_number  a" instead of "line_number a", violating the specification.]
